Average U.S. Gasoline Price Tops $4.50 to Near Four-Year High
headlines.summary: The national average price of gasoline in the United States has just topped $4.50 per gallon, the highest level since the middle of July 2022, according to data from GasBuddy, as the Iran war drains fuel inventories and sends crude prices higher. headlines.title: Pakistan Issues Emergency LNG Tender for Two Cargoes as Power Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: Pakistan on Wednesday issued a tender seeking to urgently buy two LNG cargoes for delivery later this month as the country struggles to cope with a gas and power crisis amid disrupted LNG supplies from the Middle East. headlines.title: Live: Oil Prices Fall Below $100 as Trump Say Iran War May Be Coming To End
headlines.summary: Brent crude fell 10% to $98 and WTI dropped over 12% to $89 after reports emerged that the U.S. and Iran are discussing a one-page memorandum to pause the Hormuz conflict. headlines.title: Saudi Arabia Cuts June Oil Prices by Less Than Expected as Hormuz Risks Persist
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia has reduced the official selling price of its flagship crude loading for Asia in June from the May record high, but the price cut was less than expected. Next month’s supply from the world’s top crude exporter would still see the second-highest premium to benchmarks
